news.title:

The total trade value amounted to Rs. 21.77 crores.

news.title:
India Inc secured orders worth Rs 11,994 cr spanning defence, highways, urban infra, and solar power.
BEL, GR Infraprojects, NBCC India, and HCC reported significant contracts but mixed weekly stock performance.

news.title:
Bondada Engineering surged 3.1% to Rs 285.05 on securing a BEL order.
The Rs 35.7 lakh contract for defence components will be executed in 3 months.
news.summary:

news.title:
Goldman Sachs maintains 'Buy' on Bharat Electronics (BEL) with ₹470 target, a 7% upside.
BEL's FY26 orders total ₹21,620 crore; expected to meet ₹27,000 crore guidance with large contracts pending.
news.summary:
Goldman Sachs maintains 'Buy' on Bharat Electronics (BEL) with ₹470 target, a 7% upside.
BEL's FY26 orders total ₹21,620 crore; expected to meet ₹27,000 crore guidance with large contracts pending.

news.title:
Sensex rose 568 points to 76,071, Nifty 50 up 172 to 23,581; midcaps gained 559 points.
MOIL surged 19% on production plans; Bharat Electronics rose 6% with ₹1,011 crore order.
news.summary:
Sensex rose 568 points to 76,071, Nifty 50 up 172 to 23,581; midcaps gained 559 points.
MOIL surged 19% on production plans; Bharat Electronics rose 6% with ₹1,011 crore order.

news.title:
Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) has secured orders worth Rs. 1,011 crore since February 25, 2026.
Orders include communication equipment, radar systems, fire detection systems, software solutions, and strategic components.
news.summary:
Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) has secured orders worth Rs. 1,011 crore since February 25, 2026.

news.title: BEL secures orders worth ₹10.1B, reflecting strong demand for its offerings.

Cement margins face pressure from rising fuel costs; prefers Ambuja, UltraTech.

news.title:
Geopolitical tensions, like the Iran-Israel conflict, boost Indian defence (HAL, BEL, BDL) and energy stocks (ONGC, Oil India).
Sector rotation favors defence, energy, and shipping companies amid rising crude prices and global uncertainty.

news.title:
Defence stocks face selling pressure despite strong order books and govt support; Nifty India Defence Index down 1.8%.
Short-term concerns include profit booking, supply chain issues, execution focus, but long-term prospects remain positive.
news.summary:
Defence stocks face selling pressure despite strong order books and govt support; Nifty India Defence Index down 1.8%.

news.title:
Nifty India Defence index dropped 3.5% amid profit booking; BEL, Dynamatic Tech led losses.
Mazagon Dock fell 4.2%, Hindustan Aeronautics down 1%; broader indices declined ~3%.
news.summary:

news.title:
Nifty 50 enters technical correction, down 10.1% from Jan 5 peak of 26,373.2 to 23,697 on Mar 9.
Wipro falls 26%, Jio Financial Services 24%, Asian Paints 23%. ONGC rises 16%, Bharat Electronics gains 9%.
news.summary:
Nifty 50 enters technical correction, down 10.1% from Jan 5 peak of 26,373.2 to 23,697 on Mar 9.
Wipro falls 26%, Jio Financial Services 24%, Asian Paints 23%. ONGC rises 16%, Bharat Electronics gains 9%.

news.title:
Defence stocks like HAL, BEL, BDL, and GRSE show stellar returns of up to 1750% over four years.
Experts view defence as India's new IT sector, driven by geopolitical tensions, govt push, and export growth.
news.summary:

news.title:
BEL may benefit from India's defence indigenisation policy amid geopolitical tensions.
BEL's electronics are widely used across all three defence forces, boosting prospects.

news.title:
BEL and SASMOS signed an MoU to enhance defence electronics manufacturing.
The MoU was signed in the presence of Defence Minister Rajnath Singh.

news.title:

MoU combines BEL's defence electronics expertise with Bellatrix's satellite propulsion innovation.

news.title:

news.title:
Nifty dropped 312 points, rebounding 250 points after hitting 24,600 support zone but closed below 25,000 due to geopolitical concerns.
Bharat Electronics, Hindalco gained; InterGlobe Aviation, L&T dragged indices. Bank Nifty fell 1.14%, critical support at 59,300.
news.summary:
Nifty dropped 312 points, rebounding 250 points after hitting 24,600 support zone but closed below 25,000 due to geopolitical concerns.
Bharat Electronics, Hindalco gained; InterGlobe Aviation, L&T dragged indices. Bank Nifty fell 1.14%, critical support at 59,300.

news.title:

Sensex fell 1.3%, Nifty 1.2% amid global tensions; BEL, Hindalco gained.
